literature.title: From Static to Dynamic Structures: Improving Binding Affinity Prediction with Graph-Based Deep Learning

data_repo.description: MD trajectories, scripts for MD simulation and preprocessed data for Dynaformer

code_repo.readme_description: Dynaformer is a deep learning framework that encodes molecular dynamics features to enhance protein-ligand binding affinity prediction. The repository provides pretrained models, evaluation scripts for benchmark datasets, and tools for analyzing custom protein-ligand pairs.
code_repo.known_issues: "preprocess/custom_input.py: When processing data in parallel, there may be competing data writes resulting in incorrect contents of the intermediate file (SDF), resulting in failure to output .pkl results."

study_fork.changes_summary: Fixed a typo in the pocket-graph assertion (`pro_coord` vs `pro_feat`), added safe handling for empty edge index tensors, added an `rdkit_pdb2sdf()` utility to avoid OpenBabel atom-reordering; added a full conda environment file and SLURM submission scripts.
